zh.West Ayton, Yorkshire, England: 英國越可爾韋斯特 艾頓
zh.Lincolnshire, England: 英國英格蘭林可爾史爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel De Aton Lady d'Arcy於1270年之後出生在英國英格蘭諾斯 越可爾艾頓時，她的父親William de Aton II8749歲，母親Isabel de Vere8741歲。她於1283年與Sir Roger de Darcy在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1320年7月15日之前在英國英格蘭，埋葬在英國。

zh-hans.Sproatley, Yorkshire, England: 英国越可爾斯普洛阿特利
zh-hans.West Ayton, Yorkshire, England: 英国越可爾韋斯特 艾頓

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel De Aton Lady d'Arcy于1270年之后出生在英国英格兰諾斯 越可爾艾頓时，她的父亲William de Aton II8749岁，母亲Isabel de Vere8741岁。她于1283年与Sir Roger de Darcy在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1320年7月15日之前在英国英格兰，埋葬在英国。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Aton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Hatton , with loss of initial H-.
topographic name from Middle English atte toun ‘(dweller) at the settlement’ (Old English æt thǣm tūn(e)).

variants.name: Hatton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places called Hatton (Cheshire, Derbyshire, Lincolnshire, Middlesex, Shropshire, Staffordshire, Warwickshire), Hatton Hall (Cheshire), or Cold and High Hatton (Shropshire). The placenames derive from Old English hǣth ‘heath, heather’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Heath .
English: variant of Atton with prosthetic H-, a topographic name from Middle English atte toun ‘(dweller) at the settlement’ (Old English æt thǣm tūn(e)). Compare Towne .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Chatáin, a patronymic from a personal name meaning ‘servant of (Saint) Catan’; see McHatton .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel De Aton Lady d'Arcy was born after 1270, in Ayton, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, William de Aton II, was 8749 and her mother, Isabel de Vere, was 8741. She married Sir Roger de Darcy in 1283, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died before 15 July 1320, in England, United Kingdom, and was buried in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel De Aton Lady d'Arcy est née après 1270, à Ayton, North Yorkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William de Aton II, avait 8749 ans et sa mère, Isabel de Vere, avait 8741 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Roger de Darcy en 1283, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée avant 15 juillet 1320, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterrée à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel De Aton Lady d'Arcy nach 1270 in Ayton, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William de Aton II 8749 und ihre Mutter Isabel de Vere 8741. Sie hat 1283 in Yorkshire, England Sir Roger de Darcy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb vor 15. Juli 1320 in England, United Kingdom und wurde in England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel De Aton Lady d'Arcy è nata il dopo il 1270 nel Ayton, North Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, William de Aton II, aveva 8749 anni e sua madre, Isabel de Vere, aveva 8741 anni. Lei sposato Sir Roger de Darcy nel 1283, nel Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì prima del 15 luglio 1320, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, e fu sepolta a England.

ja.Yorkshire, England: イギリスヨークシュアイア
ja.Sproatley, Yorkshire, England: イギリスヨークシュアイアスプロアトリー
ja.West Ayton, Yorkshire, England: イギリスヨークシュアイアウエスト エイトン
ja.Lincolnshire, England: イギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Isabel De Aton Lady d'Arcyが1270年以後にイギリスイギリスノース ヨークシュアイアエイトンで生まれた時，父William de Aton IIは8749歳，母Isabel de Vereは8741歳であった。1283年にイギリスヨークシュアイアでSir Roger de Darcyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1320年7月15日以前にイギリスイギリスで死去し，イギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel De Aton Lady d'Arcy(이)가 1270년 이후 영국 잉글랜드 노르스 요르크실르 에이턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Aton II(은)는 8749세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel de Vere(은)는 8741세였다. 그녀는 1283년 영국 요르크실르에서 Sir Roger de Darcy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1320년 7월 15일 이전 영국 잉글랜드에서 사망했으며 영국에 묻혔다.

mn.after 1270–Bef 15 July 1320: 1270 оноос хойш–1320 оны Долоодугаар сарын 15–наас өмнө

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel De Aton Lady d'Arcy nasceu após 1270, em Ayton, North Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William de Aton II, tinha 8749 anos e sua mãe, Isabel de Vere, tinha 8741 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Roger de Darcy em 1283, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu antes 15 de julho de 1320, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultada em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel De Aton Lady d'Arcy родилась после 1270 г. в местности аитон, норт еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени William de Aton II было 8749 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel de Vere, 8741. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Roger de Darcy в 1283 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла перед 15 июля 1320 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания и была похоронена в местности Англия.

Se casó con Sir Roger de Darcy en 1283, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió antes de 15 de julio de 1320, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido, y fue sepultada en Inglaterra.
